arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-objdump objdump 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading the debug symbol file from Microsoft's symbol server using the BuildId
# BuildId from tombstone: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try the ELF buildid format on Microsoft symbol server
echo "=== Trying _.debug/elf-buildid-sym ==="
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -1

echo "=== Trying libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid ==="
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -1
arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames. The tombstone shows frames from `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. I need to:

1. Find the debug symbols for this specific build of libcoreclr.so
2. Use them to resolve the PC addresses to function names

The BuildId is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. This is a .NET CoreCLR runtime library. Microsoft publishes symbol files for .NET runtime builds.


I can look up the symbols using this BuildId through Microsoft's symbol server or the NuGet packages that distribute .NET Android runtimes. For Android specifically, the runtime comes bundled in the `Microsoft.NETCore.App.Runtime.android-arm64` package, and I should be able to fetch the debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server using the standard format with the filename and BuildId. I've got the BuildId from the tombstone: `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. Now I'm trying different URL patterns to fetch the symbol file from Microsoft's symbol server—either using the standard ELF build ID format or trying to locate the libcoreclr.so debug symbols directly.

Before I attempt the download, let me check what tools are available on the system, then I'll try to fetch the symbols and use addr2line or llvm-symbolizer to resolve the addresses.
